_**User (2026-01-25 13:00:27Z)**_

Now give me a possibility to create so called service account. I will be accessible behind switch. By default I will see standard orders (including quick orders) with switch I can access service orders. Used for owner consumption and friends and other stuff. Also give me option to change existing order to service one and back. Ask any additional questions.

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

I'll help you implement a service account feature for managing service orders. Before I proceed, I need to clarify a few requirements:

## Questions:

1. **Switch Location**: Where should the switch to toggle between standard and service orders be located?
   - Just in the POS system?
   - Just in the admin section (orders index)?
   - Both places?

2. **Service Order Behavior**: Should service orders have any special characteristics?
   - Different pricing (discounted/free)?
   - Skip payment requirement?
   - Separate status or special badge?
   - Any other unique behavior?

3. **Visibility & Filtering**:
   - In admin orders list: Should service orders be completely hidden by default and only shown when the switch is on?
   - In POS: When the switch is on, should it show ONLY service orders, or both types?

4. **Reporting & Export**:
   - Should service orders be included in the Pohoda export?
   - Should they be tracked separately in any reports?

5. **Permissions**: Should anyone be able to create/manage service orders, or should this be restricted somehow?

6. **Order Conversion**: When converting an existing order to/from service order:
   - Should there be any restrictions (e.g., can't convert paid orders)?
   - Should we track the conversion history?

7. **Receipts**: Should service order receipts look different or have any special marking?

Please provide your preferences for these points so I can implement the feature correctly!

---

_**User (2026-01-25 13:03:12Z)**_

1. just in pos system
2. no special characteristics needed.
3. When switch is on, show only service orders. No need to handle switch in admin at all
4. no need to deal with them in reporting or exports right now
5. no restriction needed
6. avoid changing paid orders. no history tracking needed
7. no need to deal with that on receipts

```
  use Ecto.Migration

  def change do
    alter table(:orders) do
      add :is_service_order, :boolean, default: false, null: false
    end

    create index(:orders, [:is_service_order])
  end
end
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

Great! Now I have clear requirements. Let me implement a service order feature with:

1. A switch in the POS system to toggle between standard and service orders
2. When switch is on, show only service orders
3. No special pricing or behavior for service orders
4. Can convert unpaid orders between standard and service
5. No restrictions on who can create/manage them
6. No special handling in receipts, reports, or exports

Implementation plan:
1. Add a `is_service_order` boolean field to the `orders` table (migration)
2. Update the `Order` schema to include this field
3. Update the POS LiveView to:
   - Add a switch/toggle for service orders
   - Filter orders based on the switch state
   - When creating a new order, mark it as service or standard based on switch state
4. Add ability to convert existing orders (only unpaid ones) to/from service orders
5. Update the changeset to handle the new field

Let me start:

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- filePath: `/Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout/sales.ex`
- newString:

```
  def get_or_create_booking_order(booking_id, is_service_order \\ false) do
    open_orders =
      Order
      |> where([o], o.booking_id == ^booking_id)
      |> where([o], is_nil(o.deleted_at))
      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> where([o], o.is_service_order == ^is_service_order)
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: :product, payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], desc: o.inserted_at)
      |> Repo.all()
      |> Enum.map(fn order ->
        order_items = Enum.reject(order.order_items, & &1.deleted_at)
        %{order | order_items: order_items}
      end)

    case open_orders do
      [] ->
        # Check if booking has multiple guests
        booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking_id)

        case booking_guests do
          [_single_guest] ->
            # Only one guest - auto-create order
            guest_id = hd(booking_guests).guest_id

            {:ok, order} =
              create_order(%{
                "booking_id" => booking_id,
                "guest_id" => guest_id,
                "status" => "open",
                "is_service_order" => is_service_order
              })

            {:ok, get_order!(order.id)}

          multiple_guests when length(multiple_guests) > 1 ->
            # Multiple guests - need selection
            {:needs_guest_selection, multiple_guests}
        end

      [order] ->
        # Check if booking has multiple guests
        booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(order.booking_id)

        if length(booking_guests) > 1 do
          # Multiple guests but only 1 order - still allow creating more orders
          {:multiple, [order]}
        else
          # Single guest, single order
          {:ok, order}
        end

      orders when length(orders) > 1 ->
        {:multiple, orders}
    end
  end
```

- oldString:

```
  def get_or_create_booking_order(booking_id) do
    open_orders =
      Order
      |> where([o], o.booking_id == ^booking_id)
      |> where([o], is_nil(o.deleted_at))
      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: :product, payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], desc: o.inserted_at)
      |> Repo.all()
      |> Enum.map(fn order ->
        order_items = Enum.reject(order.order_items, & &1.deleted_at)
        %{order | order_items: order_items}
      end)

    case open_orders do
      [] ->
        # Check if booking has multiple guests
        booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking_id)

        case booking_guests do
          [_single_guest] ->
            # Only one guest - auto-create order
            guest_id = hd(booking_guests).guest_id

            {:ok, order} =
              create_order(%{
                "booking_id" => booking_id,
                "guest_id" => guest_id,
                "status" => "open"
              })

            {:ok, get_order!(order.id)}

          multiple_guests when length(multiple_guests) > 1 ->
            # Multiple guests - need selection
            {:needs_guest_selection, multiple_guests}
        end

      [order] ->
        # Check if booking has multiple guests
        booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(order.booking_id)

        if length(booking_guests) > 1 do
          # Multiple guests but only 1 order - still allow creating more orders
          {:multiple, [order]}
        else
          # Single guest, single order
          {:ok, order}
        end

      orders when length(orders) > 1 ->
        {:multiple, orders}
    end
  end
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="multi_replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **multi_replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- explanation: `Add service mode support to order selection page`
- replacements:

```
[map[explanation:Add service_mode parameter and filtering to mount filePath:/Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout_web/live/pos_live/order_selection.ex newString:  @impl true
  def mount(%{"booking_id" => booking_id} = params, _session, socket) do
    booking = Bookings.get_booking!(booking_id)
    service_mode = Map.get(params, "service_mode", "false") == "true"

    orders =
      CozyCheckout.Sales.Order
      |> where([o], o.booking_id == ^booking_id)
      |> where([o], is_nil(o.deleted_at))
      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> where([o], o.is_service_order == ^service_mode)
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: [], payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], asc: o.name)
      |> Repo.all()

    # Get all booking guests to show count
    all_booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking_id)

    {:ok,
     socket
     |> assign(:page_title, "Select Order")
     |> assign(:booking, booking)
     |> assign(:orders, orders)
     |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)
     |> assign(:booking_guests, [])
     |> assign(:total_guests, length(all_booking_guests))
     |> assign(:service_mode, service_mode)}
  end oldString:  @impl true
  def mount(%{"booking_id" => booking_id}, _session, socket) do
    booking = Bookings.get_booking!(booking_id)

    orders =
      CozyCheckout.Sales.Order
      |> where([o], o.booking_id == ^booking_id)
      |> where([o], is_nil(o.deleted_at))
      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: [], payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], asc: o.name)
      |> Repo.all()

    # Get all booking guests to show count
    all_booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking_id)

    {:ok,
     socket
     |> assign(:page_title, "Select Order")
     |> assign(:booking, booking)
     |> assign(:orders, orders)
     |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)
     |> assign(:booking_guests, [])
     |> assign(:total_guests, length(all_booking_guests))}
  end] map[explanation:Update create_new handler to use service_mode filePath:/Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout_web/live/pos_live/order_selection.ex newString:  @impl true
  def handle_event("create_new", _params, socket) do
    booking = socket.assigns.booking
    existing_orders = socket.assigns.orders
    service_mode = socket.assigns.service_mode

    # Get all guests for this booking
    all_booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking.id)

    # Get guest IDs that already have orders
    existing_guest_ids =
      existing_orders
      |> Enum.map(& &1.guest_id)
      |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
      |> MapSet.new()

    # Filter out guests who already have orders
    available_guests =
      Enum.reject(all_booking_guests, fn bg ->
        MapSet.member?(existing_guest_ids, bg.guest_id)
      end)

    case available_guests do
      [] ->
        # All guests already have orders
        {:noreply,
         socket
         |> put_flash(
           :error,
           "All guests already have orders. Select an existing order or add more guests to the booking."
         )
         |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)}

      [single_guest] ->
        # Only one guest available - auto-create order
        {:ok, order} = Sales.create_booking_order_for_guest(booking.id, single_guest.guest_id, service_mode)
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}

      multiple_guests when length(multiple_guests) > 1 ->
        # Multiple guests available - show selection modal
        {:noreply,
         socket
         |> assign(:show_guest_modal, true)
         |> assign(:booking_guests, multiple_guests)}
    end
  end oldString:  @impl true
  def handle_event("create_new", _params, socket) do
    booking = socket.assigns.booking
    existing_orders = socket.assigns.orders

    # Get all guests for this booking
    all_booking_guests = Bookings.list_booking_guests(booking.id)

    # Get guest IDs that already have orders
    existing_guest_ids =
      existing_orders
      |> Enum.map(& &1.guest_id)
      |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
      |> MapSet.new()

    # Filter out guests who already have orders
    available_guests =
      Enum.reject(all_booking_guests, fn bg ->
        MapSet.member?(existing_guest_ids, bg.guest_id)
      end)

    case available_guests do
      [] ->
        # All guests already have orders
        {:noreply,
         socket
         |> put_flash(
           :error,
           "All guests already have orders. Select an existing order or add more guests to the booking."
         )
         |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)}

      [single_guest] ->
        # Only one guest available - auto-create order
        {:ok, order} = Sales.create_booking_order_for_guest(booking.id, single_guest.guest_id)
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}

      multiple_guests when length(multiple_guests) > 1 ->
        # Multiple guests available - show selection modal
        {:noreply,
         socket
         |> assign(:show_guest_modal, true)
         |> assign(:booking_guests, multiple_guests)}

  end] map[explanation:Update select_guest handler to use service_mode filePath:/Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout_web/live/pos_live/order_selection.ex newString:  @impl true
  def handle_event("select_guest", %{"guest-id" => guest_id}, socket) do
    booking = socket.assigns.booking
    service_mode = socket.assigns.service_mode

    {:ok, order} = Sales.create_booking_order_for_guest(booking.id, guest_id, service_mode)

    # Reload orders to show the new one
    orders =
      CozyCheckout.Sales.Order
      |> where([o], o.booking_id == ^booking.id)
      |> where([o], is_nil(o.deleted_at))
      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> where([o], o.is_service_order == ^service_mode)
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: [], payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], asc: o.name)
      |> Repo.all()

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)
     |> assign(:orders, orders)
     |> put_flash(:info, "Order created for #{order.guest.name}")
     |> push_navigate(to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}
  end
end oldString:  @impl true

    # Reload orders to show the new one

      |> where([o], o.status in ["open", "partially_paid"])
      |> preload([:guest, booking: :guest, order_items: [], payments: []])
      |> order_by([o], asc: o.name)
      |> Repo.all()

    {:noreply,
     socket
     |> assign(:show_guest_modal, false)
     |> assign(:orders, orders)
     |> put_flash(:info, "Order created for #{order.guest.name}")
     |> push_navigate(to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}

end]]
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="replace_string_in_file"><details>

- newString:

```
  @impl true
  def handle_event("select_booking", %{"booking-id" => booking_id}, socket) do
    service_mode = socket.assigns.service_mode

    case Sales.get_or_create_booking_order(booking_id, service_mode) do
      {:ok, order} ->
        # Single guest, single order - go directly to order
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}

      {:needs_guest_selection, _guests} ->
        # Multiple guests but no orders - go to order selection to show guest modal
        {:noreply,
         push_navigate(socket,
           to: ~p"/pos/bookings/#{booking_id}/orders?service_mode=#{service_mode}"
         )}

      {:multiple, _orders} ->
        # Multiple orders exist - go to order selection
        {:noreply,
         push_navigate(socket,
           to: ~p"/pos/bookings/#{booking_id}/orders?service_mode=#{service_mode}"
         )}
    end
  end
```

- oldString:

```
  @impl true
  def handle_event("select_booking", %{"booking-id" => booking_id}, socket) do
    case Sales.get_or_create_booking_order(booking_id) do
      {:ok, order} ->
        # Single guest, single order - go directly to order
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/orders/#{order.id}")}

      {:needs_guest_selection, _guests} ->
        # Multiple guests but no orders - go to order selection to show guest modal
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/bookings/#{booking_id}/orders")}

      {:multiple, _orders} ->
        # Multiple orders exist - go to order selection
        {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/pos/bookings/#{booking_id}/orders")}
    end
  end
```

- newString:

```
  def update_order(%Order{} = order, attrs) do
    order
    |> Order.changeset(attrs)
    |> Repo.update()
  end

  @doc """
  Toggles an order between service and standard mode.
  Can only convert unpaid orders.
  """
  def toggle_service_order(%Order{} = order) do
    if order.status == "paid" do
      {:error, :cannot_convert_paid_order}
    else
      update_order(order, %{"is_service_order" => !order.is_service_order})
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Deletes an order (soft delete).
  """
  def delete_order(%Order{} = order) do
    order
    |> Ecto.Changeset.change(deleted_at: DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.truncate(:second))
    |> Repo.update()
  end
```

- oldString:

```
    |> Order.changeset(attrs)
    |> Repo.update()

  def delete_order(%Order{} = order) do
    order
    |> Ecto.Changeset.change(deleted_at: DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.truncate(:second))
    |> Repo.update()
  end
```

- newString:

```
          <.icon name="hero-arrow-left" class="w-6 h-6 text-slate-700" />
        </button>
        <div>
          <%= if @order.booking_id do %>
            <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900">
              {if @order.guest, do: @order.guest.name, else: "Unknown Guest"}
              <span :if={@order.booking.room_number} class="text-slate-500">
                - Room {@order.booking.room_number}
              </span>
            </h1>
          <% else %>
            <div class="flex items-center gap-3">
              <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900">
                📋 {@order.name}
              </h1>
              <span class="px-3 py-1 bg-info-light text-purple-700 text-sm font-semibold rounded-full">
                Standalone Order
              </span>
            </div>
          <% end %>
          <div class="flex items-center gap-3">
            <p class="text-sm text-slate-600">Order #{@order.order_number}</p>
            <%= if @order.is_service_order do %>
              <span class="px-2 py-1 bg-purple-100 text-purple-700 text-xs font-semibold rounded-full">
                Service Order
              </span>
            <% end %>
            <%= if @order.status != "paid" do %>
              <button
                phx-click="toggle_service_order"
                class="text-xs text-purple-600 hover:text-purple-800 underline"
              >
                {if @order.is_service_order, do: "Convert to Standard", else: "Convert to Service"}
              </button>
            <% end %>
          </div>
```
